title: Build with git repository with branch displays error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Support building images from git repositories at a specific branch.

Podman appears to support building images from git repositories already. This works for me, and is discussed in the [docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#building-an-image-using-a-git-repository).

However, `docker build` supports building a specific branch of a git repository by appending `#branchname` to the end of the URL. For example, this will build the `cap21and40` branch instead of the default branch:

```
docker build -t stellar/quickstart:cap21and40 https://github.com/leighmcculloch/stellar--docker-stellar-core-horizon.git#cap21and40
```

When I attempt to do the same thing with podman I get an error:

```
podman build -t stellar/quickstart:cap21and40 -f Dockerfile https://github.com/leighmcculloch/stellar--docker-stellar-core-horizon.git#cap21and40
Error: no FROM statement found
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run the podman command above.

2. Watch it fail.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: no FROM statement found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected it to start building the git repository at the given branch.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

N/A

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.3.1

Go Version:   go1.17
Built:        Mon Aug 30 12:15:26 2021

Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Fri Aug 20 12:36:14 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, this is the latest version available on brew.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

macOS 11.5.2. `podman machine init` was used to setup the vm.

comments.body: @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat Needs to be moved to Buildah, I think

It looks like buildah supports it, so maybe this is a bug in buildahs implementation or podman's use of buildahs? https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/aff9f6beb2a3238938120b5cab8d2e65408b149a/define/types.go#L162

Ok I think this could be a bug in buildah.

At the following line buildah only considers a URL and Git repo if it starts with git:// or ends in .git, but a URL containing a branch will have the branch after the .git. buildah should parse the URL and check that .git is the end of the path excluding the anchor/hash and anything that trails. 

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/aff9f6beb2a3238938120b5cab8d2e65408b149a/define/types.go#L114

I'll open a PR with an attempted fix.
